info.name: gong-entrypoints — callschedulers
info.description: Triggers for the callschedulers module entry points, exposed by the gong-entrypoints app. Requests are grouped into a folder per entrypoint. Set the active Postman Environment (callschedulers.localhost) to point {{baseUrl}} at the running gong-entrypoints app (always localhost:8080). The downstream CallScheduler must be running locally on port 8091 (IntelliJ 'CallScheduler' run config). NOTE: send-calendar-event now auto-generates a valid CallSchedulingRequest per send — a body is optional, and when supplied only the event IDs/timestamps are overridden. Loops fire 1/second.

item.name: send-calendar-event
item.description: Entrypoint #2 — send a calendar event through the core scheduling pipeline. Submits a CallSchedulingRequest (type CALENDAR_EVENT) to the troubleshooter, which puts it on the CALL_SCHEDULING_REQUESTS Kafka topic. The consumer drives the full pipeline: distributed lock → callSchedulingRequestsService.callIncomingCalendarInviteHandler() → validation → scheduling decision. Breakpoint: CallSchedulingRequestsConsumer.accept() line 87. The endpoint AUTO-GENERATES a valid payload per send: a body is optional (no body → fully generated; body present → used as base with a fresh providerEventId/iCalUID + current timestamps overridden per send). This makes loop=N produce N genuinely-processed events (each gets fresh IDs, so no TOO_OLD_REQUEST dedup rejection); the loop fires 1/second. Seed: companyId=9001, userId=501, emailAddress=alice@acme-corp.com, provider=Google, callCreationMechanism=CALENDAR_INGESTER (the largest happy path — writes updated_calendar_event, full validator chain). Use a real TLD (.com) — the validator rejects .test emails.

item.name: auto-generate — no body (happy path smoke test)

request.description: No body — the endpoint auto-generates a full valid CallSchedulingRequest with a random providerEventId/iCalUID and current timestamps, pinned to the seed (company 9001, user 501, alice@acme-corp.com, CALENDAR_INGESTER). Expect 200, body 'Done'. Check the callscheduler logs for resolution=NEW_CALL.

Fires 10 auto-generated events, 1/second. Each gets a fresh providerEventId/iCalUID, so all 10 are processed downstream (no TOO_OLD_REQUEST dedup rejection). Verify 10 distinct rows in call_scheduler.updated_calendar_event WHERE company_id=9001.

item.name: A — CALENDAR_EVENT, future meeting (happy path smoke test)

body.raw:
{
  "companyId": 9001,
  "callSchedulingEventType": "CALENDAR_EVENT",
  "callCreationMechanism": "CALENDAR_SYNC",
  "calendarPayload": {
    "userId": 501,
    "emailAddress": "alice@acme-corp.com",
    "provider": "Google",
    "providerEventId": "smoke-test-event-001",
    "iCalUID": "smoke-test-event-001@google.com",
    "recurringEventId": null,
    "etag": "1234567890",
    "organizer": {
      "name": "Alice",
      "emailAddress": "alice@acme-corp.com",
      "responseStatus": "ACCEPTED",
      "role": "ORGANIZER"
    },
    "creator": null,
    "invitees": [
      {
        "name": "Bob",
        "emailAddress": "bob@acme-corp.com",
        "responseStatus": "ACCEPTED",
        "role": "PARTICIPANT"
      }
    ],
    "startTime": "2026-08-01T10:00:00Z",
    "endTime": "2026-08-01T11:00:00Z",
    "createTime": "2026-07-01T09:00:00Z",
    "lastModifiedTime": "2026-07-01T09:00:00Z",
    "originalStartTime": null,
    "summary": "Smoke Test Meeting",
    "description": "https://zoom.us/j/123456789",
    "location": null,
    "additionalMeetingUrls": [],
    "isPrivateOrConfidential": false,
    "isAllDay": false,
    "isCancelled": false,
    "isRecurrent": false,
    "isCrmIntegrationEnabled": false,
    "isMeetingIndexed": false
  }
}

request.description: Path A — standard CALENDAR_SYNC event, future meeting, non-recurring. The event goes onto the Kafka topic and the consumer drives it through the full scheduling pipeline. Expect 200, body 'Done'. companyId=9001 (Acme Corp), userId=501 (Alice), emailAddress=alice@acme-corp.com — from seed-dialers-local.sql. The Zoom URL in description exercises the meeting-URL extraction path.

request.description: Path B — same iCalUID as path A but isCancelled=true. Exercises the CalendarCancelEvent branch in toCalendarEvent() and the cancellation leg of the scheduling pipeline. Run path A first to create the scheduled call, then this to cancel it. Expect 200, body 'Done'.

request.description: Fires the calendar event N times (1/second) with an explicit body. The body is the base; each iteration's providerEventId/iCalUID and timestamps are overridden, so every send is a clean schedule attempt.

item.name: auto-generate — no body, loop until stopped (loop=true, 1/sec)

request.description: Fires auto-generated events 1/second until stopped. This request BLOCKS the connection until you hit 'send-calendar-event — stop loop' from another Postman tab / shell. Each iteration gets a fresh providerEventId/iCalUID, so all sends are processed (no TOO_OLD_REQUEST dedup rejection). Returns 'Loop stopped after N iterations' once stopped.
Stops an in-progress loop=true run.

item.name: path coverage
item.description: Scenario-driven path coverage. Each Tier-A request is body-less and picks a target pipeline path with ?scenario=NAME; the endpoint auto-generates a payload that deterministically walks that path (fresh event IDs per send). Tier-B scenarios are stateful — fire the paired '…-setup' (a NEW_CALL) then '…-trigger', both sharing a pinned ?iCalUID, so the second send acts on the row the first created. See the README 'Path coverage' section for the breakpoint each scenario reaches. Requires seed-callscheduler-local.sql applied to honeyfy_dev (adds the should_record=FALSE user for USER_NOT_MARKED_FOR_RECORDING and the second should_record=TRUE user for CHANGED_OWNER).

request.description: Happy path. Breakpoint: CallBuilder — 'Entered new call flow'. Resolution NEW_CALL.
isPrivateOrConfidential=true. Breakpoint: CheckEventRelevance.validate — 'Event is private or confidential'. Resolution PRIVATE_OR_CONFIDENTIAL_EVENT.
iCalUID=null. Breakpoint: CheckEventRelevance.validate — 'Missing IcalID'. Resolution NO_ICAL_ID.
startTime=null. Breakpoint: CheckEventRelevance.validate — 'Missing event start time'. Resolution NO_START_TIME.
endTime=null. Breakpoint: CheckEventRelevance.validate — 'Missing event end time'. Resolution NO_END_TIME.
startTime = now − 2h (end likewise past). Breakpoint: CheckEventRelevance.validate — 'Obsolete event'. Resolution OBSOLETE_EVENT.
description has no meeting URL, empty additionalMeetingUrls. Breakpoint: CheckUrlValidity → CallInDetailsService.validate — 'Invite without call in details'. Resolution NO_CALL_IN_DETAILS.
organizer email = nobody@acme-corp.com (maps to no user). Breakpoint: CheckOrganizer.validate — 'Could not find an owner'. Resolution CANNOT_IDENTIFY_CALL_OWNER.

provider=GoogleApps (invalid shortName; valid is Google). Breakpoint: CallSchedulingRequestsService.internalIncomingCalendarInviteHandler — throws IllegalArgumentException 'Unknown provider shortName'.
userId=999999 (unseeded). Breakpoint: CallSchedulingRequestsService.createCalendarOwner — throws RuntimeException 'User not found'.

All-internal meeting. Breakpoint: CheckInternalMeetingAllowed.validate. Only yields INTERNAL_MEETING_RECORDING_DISABLED when the company's recordInternalMeetings=false; otherwise falls through to NEW_CALL (then it's a Tier-C company-settings path — see README).

userId/organizer = 503 (carol@acme-corp.com, should_record=FALSE from the expanded seed). Breakpoint: IncomingEventHandler.handleMeetingRequestForKnownUser — 'Invite disregarded … user marked as not synced'. Resolution USER_NOT_MARKED_FOR_RECORDING.
Step 1: create the row for iCalUID=too-old-001@google.com. Then fire the '-trigger' request.
Step 2: same iCalUID, lastModifiedTime = now − 2h. Breakpoint: IncomingEventHandler.updateEventIfNotTooOld — 'Event is too old'. Resolution TOO_OLD_REQUEST.
Step 1: create the row for iCalUID=reschedule-001@google.com. Then fire the '-trigger' request.
Step 2: same iCalUID, startTime shifted +3h. Breakpoint: CallBuilder — reschedule branch. Resolution RESCHEDULED.

item.name: CHANGED_OWNER — setup (NEW_CALL as user A, pinned iCalUID)

request.description: Step 1: create the row as user A (501, alice). Then fire the '-trigger' request.

item.name: CHANGED_OWNER — trigger (same iCalUID, owner B = user 502)

request.description: Step 2: same iCalUID, organizer/userId = 502 (bob@acme-corp.com, a second should_record=TRUE seed user). Breakpoint: CallBuilder — changed-owner branch. Resolution CHANGED_OWNER.
Step 1: create the scheduled call for iCalUID=cancel-001@google.com. Then fire the '-trigger' request.
Step 2: same iCalUID, isCancelled=true. Breakpoint: CallBuilder.cancelInviteesAndPossiblyCall → cancelCall — 'Marking call as cancelled'. Resolution CANCELLED.

Emits an EMAIL_EVENT (MIME rawMessage) with callCreationMechanism=CALENDAR_SYNC_EMAIL — drives the handleEmailEventType arm of CallSchedulingRequestsConsumer.accept. Breakpoint: IncomingEmailInviteHandler.handleIncomingEmail → handleGeneralEmailEvent. From/ORGANIZER pinned to seed user alice@acme-corp.com.
EMAIL_EVENT with callCreationMechanism=OPT_IN_EMAIL. Breakpoint: IncomingEmailInviteHandler.handleIncomingEmailForKnownCompany — isOptInEmail() branch (opt-in reply logic).
EMAIL_EVENT with callCreationMechanism=COORDINATOR_EMAIL. Breakpoint: CallSchedulingRequestsService.callIncomingEmailInviteHandler — COORDINATOR_EMAIL branch (updateOneTimeMeetingIfNeeded).

item.name: cancel-blacklisted-calls
item.description: Entrypoint #1 — cancel blacklisted calls for a company. Cancels any scheduled call recordings whose participants are on the company's blacklist. SELF-CONTAINED — no setup step: the operation is a no-op if no blacklisted calls are currently scheduled for companyId=9001 (check the logs for 'blacklist' after firing). Downstream: PUT /troubleshooting/blacklistedCalls/cancelBlacklistedForCompany on CallScheduler (TroubleshootingBlacklistedCalls.cancelBlacklistedForCompany).

query.description: The company whose blacklisted scheduled calls should be cancelled.

request.description: Cancels blacklisted calls targeting localhost:8091. Expect 200 with body 'Done — cancelled blacklisted calls for companyId=9001'.

item.name: cancel-call
item.description: Entrypoint #3 — cancel a specific scheduled call by callId. Exercises ScheduledCallsActionsController.cancelScheduledCallByOwner() → CancelCallService.cancelByOwnerScheduledCall(). SELF-CONTAINED: run the two requests in order top-to-bottom — step 1 creates a fresh call and captures its callId into the {{cancelCallId}} collection variable, step 2 cancels that exact call. No manual DB lookup or copy/paste. Downstream: POST /scheduledCallsActions/cancelScheduledCallByOwner.

exec: // Capture the freshly-created callId so step 2 can cancel it — no DB lookup needed.
pm.test('setup created a call and returned a callId', function () {

request.description: Creates a fresh MANUAL scheduled call (company 9001, seed user 501). The test script parses 'callId=<id>' from the response and stores it in the {{cancelCallId}} collection variable. Run this first, then step 2.

query.description: Auto-filled from step 1 (the setup request). Run step 1 first, or set {{cancelCallId}} manually.

request.description: Cancels the call created in step 1 (via the {{cancelCallId}} variable). Expect 200 with body 'Done — cancelled callId=<id> for companyId=9001'.

item.name: restore-call
item.description: Entrypoint #4 — restore a previously cancelled scheduled call. Exercises ScheduledCallsActionsController.restoreCancelledCallByOwner() → RestoreCancelledCallService. SELF-CONTAINED round-trip: run the three requests in order top-to-bottom — step 1 creates a fresh call and captures {{restoreCallId}}, step 2 cancels it, step 3 restores it. No manual DB lookup or copy/paste. Downstream: POST /scheduledCallsActions/restoreCancelledCallByOwner.

exec: // Capture the freshly-created callId so steps 2 and 3 act on the same call.
pm.test('setup created a call and returned a callId', function () {

request.description: Creates a fresh MANUAL scheduled call and stores its callId in {{restoreCallId}}. Run this first.

query.description: The call created in step 1. A call must be cancelled before it can be restored.

request.description: Cancels the call from step 1 so there is something to restore. Expect 200 'Done — cancelled callId=<id> for companyId=9001'.

query.description: Auto-filled from step 1. The call must have been cancelled (step 2) first.

request.description: Restores the call cancelled in step 2 (via {{restoreCallId}}). Expect 200 with body 'Done — restored callId=<id> for companyId=9001'.

item.name: cancel-internal-meetings
item.description: Entrypoint #5 — cancel all internal meeting recordings for a company. SELF-CONTAINED: run the two steps in order — step 1 creates an all-internal scheduled call via send-calendar-event (INTERNAL_MEETING_DISABLED scenario); step 2 cancels all internal calls for company 9001 (i.e. what it just created). Exercises ScheduledCallsActionsController.cancelScheduledInternalMeetingsCallsRecordings() → CancelCallService. NOTE: step 1 only creates an internal call when company 9001 has recordInternalMeetings=true — check callscheduler logs for resolution=NEW_CALL. If the company flag is false the scenario resolves INTERNAL_MEETING_RECORDING_DISABLED and nothing is created (then step 2 is a no-op). Downstream: POST /scheduledCallsActions/cancelScheduledInternalMeetingsCallsRecordings.

item.name: 1. setup — create an internal meeting (INTERNAL_MEETING_DISABLED scenario)

request.description: Creates an all-internal scheduled call (all invitees from @acme-corp.com) for company 9001. Requires company 9001 to have recordInternalMeetings=true; if true, resolution=NEW_CALL and a SCHEDULED call appears in operational.public.call. Step 2 then cancels it. Breakpoint: CheckInternalMeetingAllowed.validate → CallBuilder.

Cancels all internal meeting scheduled calls for company 9001. Expect 200 with body 'Done — cancelled internal meeting recordings for companyId=9001'. Verify: SELECT id, status, capture_skip_code FROM public.call WHERE companyid=9001 AND capture_skip_code='INTERNAL_MEETING' ORDER BY id DESC.

item.name: cancel-inactive-users
item.description: Entrypoint #6 — cancel inactive users' scheduled calls and rescan co-invitees' calendars. The offboarding cleanup path. SELF-CONTAINED — no setup step needed: the operation is a no-op if no users have been inactive for ≥num-of-days (check callscheduler logs for 'inactive'). Only CALENDAR_INGESTER mechanism calls are affected. Exercises TroubleshootingInactiveUsersCalls.cancelInactiveUsersCallsAndRescanOtherCalendarsForCompany(). Downstream: POST /troubleshooting/inactive-users-calls/cancel-inactive-users-calls-and-rescan-other-calendars-for-company.

query.description: Users inactive for this many days are treated as inactive. Default: 7.

request.description: Cancels calls owned by users inactive ≥7 days, then rescans co-invitees. Expect 200. No-op if no matching inactive users are found.

item.name: run-recurring-scheduled-task
item.description: Entrypoint — the recurring-events scheduled task (cron/batch mechanism). Zero-arg smoke test: runs RecurringEventsCallScheduledTask.runTask() downstream, scanning calendar_recurring_event and scheduling upcoming occurrences. Fires even against an empty table (scans, finds nothing) — proves the mechanism end-to-end. Downstream: POST /troubleshooting/recurring-meetings/run-scheduled-task.

request.description: Runs the recurring-events scheduled task once. Expect 200 with body 'Done'. Set a breakpoint on RecurringEventsCallScheduledTask.runTask() to watch the cron mechanism fire.

item.name: run-recurring-scheduled-task-for-event
item.description: Entrypoint — run the recurring-events scheduled task scoped to a single iCalUID. SELF-CONTAINED: uses {{recurringICalUID}} set by the restore-recurring-event folder's step 1. Run that setup first, then fire step 1 here to regenerate upcoming occurrences for the series. Alternatively set {{recurringICalUID}} manually to an existing ical_uid in calendar_recurring_event. Downstream: POST /troubleshooting/recurring-meetings/run-scheduled-task-for-specific-event (TroubleshootingRecurringEmailScheduling.runRecurringEventsCallScheduledTaskForSpecificEvent).

query.description: Auto-filled from restore-recurring-event step 1 ({{recurringICalUID}}). Or set manually to an ical_uid in calendar_recurring_event for companyId=9001.
Whether to include cancelled occurrences (default false).

request.description: Runs the recurring-events task for the series in {{recurringICalUID}}. Breakpoint: RecurringEventsCallScheduledTask.runTask() or RecurringEventService.executeRecurringEventsCallScheduledTask(). Expect 200 'Done'. No-op if the series has no upcoming occurrences in the look-ahead window — widen num-of-days if needed.

item.name: cancel-recurring-event
item.description: Entrypoint — manually cancel a recurring event series. SELF-CONTAINED: run the two steps in order — step 1 creates a recurring series via send-calendar-event with a pinned iCalUID (stores {{recurringICalUID}}), step 2 cancels that series and all its upcoming calls. Parallel to cancel-call for one-time events. Downstream: POST /troubleshooting/recurring-meetings/manually-cancel-recurring-event (TroubleshootingRecurringEmailScheduling.manuallyCancelRecurringEvent).

exec: // Pin the iCalUID so step 2 can cancel the exact series created here.
pm.test('setup fired a recurring series event', function () {

request.description: Fires a NEW_CALL calendar event pinned to iCalUID=recurring-cancel-001@google.com. The test script stores that UID in {{recurringICalUID}} so step 2 can cancel it without any manual lookup. Breakpoint: CallBuilder — NEW_CALL flow. Verify: SELECT * FROM call_scheduler.calendar_recurring_event WHERE company_id=9001 AND ical_uid='recurring-cancel-001@google.com'.

query.description: Auto-filled from step 1. The iCalUID of the series to cancel.

request.description: Cancels the recurring series created in step 1. Marks it cancelled in calendar_recurring_event and cancels all associated upcoming scheduled calls. Expect 200 with 'Cancelled event: 1, calls: N'. Run restore-recurring-event (steps 1–3) to undo.

item.name: restore-recurring-event
item.description: Entrypoint — restore a cancelled recurring event series. SELF-CONTAINED round-trip: run the three steps in order — step 1 creates the series, step 2 cancels it, step 3 restores it. Uses {{recurringICalUID}} set by step 1. After restoring, run run-recurring-scheduled-task-for-event to regenerate upcoming call occurrences. Parallel to restore-call for one-time events. Downstream: POST /troubleshooting/recurring-meetings/restore-cancelled-recurring-main-event (TroubleshootingRecurringEmailScheduling.restoreCancelledMainEvent).

exec: // Pin the iCalUID so steps 2 and 3 act on the same series.
pm.test('setup fired a recurring series event', function () {

request.description: Fires a NEW_CALL calendar event pinned to iCalUID=recurring-restore-001@google.com. The test script stores that UID in {{recurringICalUID}}. Breakpoint: CallBuilder — NEW_CALL flow.

query.description: The series created in step 1. Must be cancelled before it can be restored.

request.description: Cancels the series from step 1 so there is something to restore. Expect 'Cancelled event: 1, calls: N'.

query.description: Auto-filled from step 1. The iCalUID of the previously cancelled series.

request.description: Restores the recurring series cancelled in step 2. Clears the cancelled flag in calendar_recurring_event. Expect 200 'Done — restored recurring series ical-uid=recurring-restore-001@google.com for companyId=9001'. Run run-recurring-scheduled-task-for-event after this to regenerate upcoming scheduled calls.

item.name: delete-updated-calendar-events
item.description: Entrypoint — delete old rows from updated_calendar_event. Every send-calendar-event call writes a row there; this task prunes rows older than days-back to keep the table clean during local loop testing. Default days-back=1 clears everything written today. Downstream: POST /troubleshooting/updated-calendar-events/run-scheduled-task (TroubleshootingDeleteUpdatedCalendarEvents.runDeleteUpdatedCalendarEventsScheduledTask).

query.description: Delete rows older than this many days. Default 1 — clears all rows written more than 24h ago.

request.description: Prunes updated_calendar_event rows older than 1 day. Run after a send-calendar-event loop session to keep the table manageable. Expect 200 with body 'Done'.

item.name: schedule-call-manually
item.description: Entrypoint #12 — schedule a new call manually (use case A4). The direct scheduling path that bypasses calendar and email ingestion — stands in for the Gong UI action a user takes to say 'record this meeting'. Writes a MANUAL-mechanism row into operational.call. This is the second independent source of new scheduled calls (alongside send-calendar-event), so it's the fastest way to stream fresh MANUAL rows into a local DB. Each send uses a fresh callId + current-relative start/end times, so loop=N produces N distinct scheduled calls. Downstream: POST /scheduledCallsActions/scheduleNewCallManually?companyId=… (ScheduledCallsActionsController.scheduleNewCallManually), body ManualSchedulingCallDetails. Seed: company 9001, workspace 1001, user 501 (alice@acme-corp.com), provider ZOOM (the enabled seed provider). Breakpoint: ManualSchedulingCallService.scheduleNewCallManually().

request.description: Creates one MANUAL scheduled call pinned to the seed (company 9001, workspace 1001, user 501, provider ZOOM). The trigger builds a full ManualSchedulingCallDetails with a fresh random callId and start=now+1h / end=now+2h. Expect 200 with body 'Done — scheduled manual callId=<id> for companyId=9001'. Verify: SELECT * FROM operational.call WHERE company_id=9001 AND call_creation_mechanism='MANUAL' ORDER BY id DESC.

query.description: Fires 10 times, 1/second. Each gets a fresh callId, so all 10 create distinct scheduled calls.

request.description: Streams 10 distinct MANUAL scheduled calls, 1/second. Verify 10 new rows in operational.call WHERE company_id=9001 AND call_creation_mechanism='MANUAL'.

query.description: Loops 1/second until '/stop' is called from another tab. BLOCKS the connection until stopped.

request.description: Fires MANUAL scheduled calls 1/second until stopped. This request BLOCKS until you hit 'schedule-call-manually — stop loop' from another Postman tab / shell. Returns 'Loop stopped after N iterations' once stopped.
Stops an in-progress loop=true run.

variable.description: Auto-populated by the cancel-call folder's setup step (step 1). The callId that gets cancelled.
Auto-populated by the restore-call folder's setup step (step 1). The callId that gets cancelled then restored.

Auto-populated by the cancel-recurring-event or restore-recurring-event folder's setup step (step 1). The iCalUID of the recurring series being exercised. Also consumed by run-recurring-scheduled-task-for-event.
